html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Formal title meaning 'Imperially Approved Four Treasuries,' referring to the official compilation of Chinese literature.
  • 子部十二 - Refers to the twelfth category of the Four Treasuries, which is the 'Miscellaneous' section.
  • 夷坚志 - The title of the text, which means 'The Records of the Strange.'
  • 小説家二【异闻之属】 - A classification indicating that this text belongs to the second category of the 'Miscellaneous' section, specifically dealing with strange tales.
  • 提要 - A brief introduction or summary of the text.
  • 【臣】等谨案 - A formal expression used in imperial documents, meaning 'We officials respectfully examine.'
  • 容斋随笔 - The title of another work by the same author, which means 'Random Notes from the Rongzhai Studio.'
  • 列子夷坚志事 - Refers to the story of the *Liezi* (a Daoist text) and the *Yijian Zhi* (a collection of strange tales).
  • 伯益知而名之 - A phrase from the *Liezi* meaning 'Bo Yi knew it and named it.'
  • 夷坚闻而志之 - A phrase from the *Liezi* meaning 'Yijian heard of it and recorded it.'
  • 珍禽异兽 - Means 'precious birds and exotic animals.'
  • 仙鬼诸事 - Means 'various matters concerning immortals and ghosts.'
  • 唐义原尉张慎素 - Refers to Zhang Shensu, a Tang dynasty official who had a work titled *Yijian Lu* (Records of the Strange).
  • 陈振孙书录解题 - The title of a bibliographic work by Chen Zhensun, which means 'Explanations of Book Records.'
  • 甲至癸二百卷 - Refers to the first 200 volumes of the *Yijian Zhi*, divided into 10 sections labeled A to J.
  • 支甲至支癸一百卷 - Refers to the next 100 volumes, divided into 10 sections labeled A to J.
  • 三甲至三癸一百卷 - Refers to the next 100 volumes, divided into 10 sections labeled A to J.
  • 四甲四乙二十卷 - Refers to the last 20 volumes, divided into 2 sections labeled A and B.
  • 赵与峕宾退録 - The title of a work by Zhao Yushi, which means 'Record of Returning Guest.'
  • 夷坚志三十二编 - Refers to the 32 volumes of the *Yijian Zhi*.
  • 凡三十一序不相重 - Means 'There are 31 prefaces, none of which are repeated.'
  • 各节録其序之大畧 - Means 'Each section records the main points of the preface.'
  • 颇为详备 - Means 'quite detailed and comprehensive.'
  • 此本仅存自甲至戊五十卷 - Means 'This version only contains the first 50 volumes, from A to E.'
  • 标题但曰夷坚志 - Means 'The title is simply *Yijian Zhi*.'
  • 失载其原序 - Means 'The original preface is not included.'
  • 证以与峕之 - Means 'Compared with Zhao Yushi's version.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - An official compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - One of the four major categories of the *Siku Quanshu*, which includes various types of literature.
  • 夷坚志 - A collection of strange tales compiled by Hong Mai during the Song dynasty.
  • 小説家二【异闻之属】 - A category within the *Siku Quanshu* that includes works dealing with unusual and supernatural events.
  • 提要 - A brief introduction or summary often found in bibliographic works.
  • 【臣】等谨案 - A formal expression used in imperial documents, indicating that the following text is an official examination or report.
  • 容斋随笔 - Another work by Hong Mai, a collection of notes and observations on various topics.
  • 列子夷坚志事 - A reference to the *Liezi* (a Daoist text) and the *Yijian Zhi* (a collection of strange tales).
  • 伯益知而名之 - A phrase from the *Liezi* meaning 'Bo Yi knew it and named it.'
  • 夷坚闻而志之 - A phrase from the *Liezi* meaning 'Yijian heard of it and recorded it.'
  • 珍禽异兽 - Precious birds and exotic animals, often mentioned in mythological and legendary texts.
  • 仙鬼诸事 - Matters concerning immortals and ghosts, common themes in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 唐义原尉张慎素 - Zhang Shensu, a Tang dynasty official who had a work titled *Yijian Lu* (Records of the Strange).
  • 陈振孙书录解题 - A bibliographic work by Chen Zhensun, which provides detailed information about various books.
  • 甲至癸二百卷 - The first 200 volumes of the *Yijian Zhi*, divided into 10 sections.
  • 支甲至支癸一百卷 - The next 100 volumes, divided into 10 sections.
  • 三甲至三癸一百卷 - The next 100 volumes, divided into 10 sections.
  • 四甲四乙二十卷 - The last 20 volumes, divided into 2 sections.
  • 赵与峕宾退録 - A work by Zhao Yushi, which records various events and observations.
  • 夷坚志三十二编 - The 32 volumes of the *Yijian Zhi*.
  • 凡三十一序不相重 - There are 31 prefaces, none of which are repeated.
  • 各节録其序之大畧 - Each section records the main points of the preface.
  • 颇为详备 - Quite detailed and comprehensive.
  • 此本仅存自甲至戊五十卷 - This version only contains the first 50 volumes, from A to E.
  • 标题但曰夷坚志 - The title is simply *Yijian Zhi*.
  • 失载其原序 - The original preface is not included.
  • 证以与峕之 - Compared with Zhao Yushi's version.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - Official compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - Category within the *Siku Quanshu*.
  • 夷坚志 - Collection of strange tales compiled by Hong Mai during the Song dynasty.
  • 容斋随笔 - Work by Hong Mai, a collection of notes and observations.
  • 列子 - Daoist text.
  • 山海经 - Classic of Mountains and Seas, a text containing myths and legends.
  • 唐义原尉张慎素 - Tang dynasty official who had a work titled *Yijian Lu* (Records of the Strange).
  • 陈振孙 - Author of *Book Records*.
  • 赵与峕 - Author of *Record of Returning Guest*.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 所載乃支甲至支戊 - The text contains entries from '支甲' to '支戊.'
  • 非其正集 - These are not the main or primary collections.
  • 惟與峕記 - They are only mentioned in the records of the time.
  • 支丙作支景 - Refers to '支丙' being referred to as '支景,' possibly to avoid a taboo name.
  • 謂避其曽祖之嫌名 - Explains that '支丙' was changed to '支景' to avoid the name of a great-grandfather, which was considered taboo.
  • 而此仍作丙 - Indicates that in some versions, '丙' is still used instead of '景.'
  • 疑傳冩者所改也 - Suggests that the change might have been made by the scribes.
  • 謂所藏之本有百卷 - States that the version he possessed had a hundred volumes.
  • 謂應麟所藏乃支甲至三甲 - Indicates that Hu Yinglin's version only contained entries from '支甲' to '三甲.'
  • 此殆即胡氏之本而又佚其半欤 - Suggests that this might be the same version as Hu's, but half of it is missing.
  • 不知為志中之一集 - Indicates that he did not know it was one of the sections in the collection.
  • 乃云夷堅志本四百二十卷 - States that he claimed 'Yi Jian Zhi' originally had 420 volumes.
  • 今行者五十一卷 - Indicates that the current version has 51 volumes.
  • 蓋病其煩芜而删之 - Suggests that the reduction was due to the text being too lengthy and disorganized.
  • 則悮之甚矣 - Indicates that this is a significant error.
  • 讥迈为谬用其心 - Criticizes Ma for misusing his intentions.
  • 其説颇正 - Indicates that his criticism is quite accurate.
  • 謂迈欲修國史 - Suggests that Ma wanted to write a national history.
  • 借此以練習其筆 - Indicates that he used this as a way to practice his writing skills.
  • 似乎曲为之词 - Suggests that this explanation seems somewhat evasive.
  • 往往可資採録 - Suggests that they can often be used for reference.
  • 亦多足為勸戒 - Indicates that they can also serve as moral lessons.
  • 非盡無益於人心者 - Suggests that not all of it is useless for the heart and mind.
  • 小説一家 - Refers to the category of 'novels' or 'fiction.'
  • 亦何必拘于方隅 - Suggests that it is not necessary to be confined to a particular region.
  • 獨为迈书责欤 - Indicates that it is not fair to blame Ma alone for the text.
  • 乾隆四十三年五月恭校上 - Indicates the date of the imperial review and publication.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夷堅志 - Refers to 'Yi Jian Zhi,' a famous collection of strange tales compiled by Gan Bao during the Song Dynasty.
  • 支甲至支戊 - Refers to the various sections of 'Yi Jian Zhi,' specifically the branches from '支甲' to '支戊.'
  • 支丙作支景 - A specific example of a name change to avoid a taboo.
  • 胡應麟 - A Ming dynasty scholar known for his literary criticism.
  • 周亮工 - A Qing dynasty scholar and official.
  • 朱國祯 - A Ming dynasty scholar and official.
  • 陳振孫 - A Song dynasty scholar and critic.
  • 張世南 - A Song dynasty scholar and official.
  • 乾隆四十三年 - Refers to the 43rd year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign (1778).

Proper Nouns

  • 夷堅志 - Yi Jian Zhi (Strange Tales of Yi Jian)
  • 支甲至支戊 - Sections of Yi Jian Zhi from '支甲' to '支戊'
  • 胡應麟 - Hu Yinglin
  • 周亮工 - Zhou Lianggong
  • 朱國祯 - Zhu Guozhen
  • 陳振孫 - Chen Zhensun
  • 張世南 - Zhang Shinan
  • 乾隆四十三年 - 1778 (43rd year of Qianlong's reign)
  • 紀昀 - Ji Yun
  • 陸錫熊 - Lu Xixiong
  • 孫士毅 - Sun Shiyi
  • 陸費墀 - Lu Feichi

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 信马行 - Letting the horse wander.
  • 扣门将托宿 - Knocking on the door to ask for lodging.
  • 惊语之曰 - Surprised and said to him.
  • 此地近山多狼虎 - This place is near the mountains and has many wolves and tigers.
  • 岂宜夜涉 - Is it suitable to travel at night?
  • 适不意迷途 - Just happened to lose my way unintentionally.
  • 敢求栖寓一席之地 - Dare to ask for a place to stay.
  • 但不知为何大官第宅 - But I don't know whose mansion this is.
  • 是河中府尹张相公之居 - It is the residence of Zhang Xianggong, the governor of Hedong Prefecture.
  • 相公薨亡 - The governor has passed away.
  • 唯夫人在 - Only the lady is here.
  • 须禀命乃可 - Permission must be sought.
  • 遂入白之 - He then went in to inform her.
  • 少顷延客相见 - After a short while, she invited him to meet.
  • 明烛盈前 - Bright candles lit up the room.
  • 已罗列杯盘 - The cups and dishes were already arranged.
  • 夫人容色端妍 - The lady's appearance was graceful and beautiful.
  • 冠服华盛 - Her attire was splendid.
  • 便与同宴 - They began to dine together.
  • 侍儿歌舞之妙 - The maidservants' dancing and singing were exquisite.
  • 目所未覩 - Never seen before.
  • 钱自谓竒遇若游仙都 - Qian felt it was a strange encounter like traveling to the fairy realm.
  • 情思荡摇 - His emotions were stirred.
  • 莫知身世之所留 - He didn't know where he belonged.
  • 拱手敬坐 - He stood respectfully.
  • 不轻交一谈 - He did not easily engage in conversation.
  • 诸人以为野戅 - The others thought he was rustic and foolish.
  • 相视笑侮 - They looked at each other and laughed mockingly.
  • 罢席就枕 - After the meal, he went to bed.
  • 俄而烛至 - Soon after, the candles were lit again.
  • 夫人者复来 - The lady came again.
  • 众拥之登床 - The others surrounded her and helped her onto the bed.
  • 钱趋下辞避 - Qian hurriedly stepped down to avoid.
  • 强之再三 - She insisted repeatedly.
  • 于是共寝 - They finally shared the bed.
  • 明旦留之饭 - The next morning, they kept him for breakfast.
  • 钱本漂泊旅人 - Originally a wandering traveler.
  • 既称惬怀抱 - He was very satisfied.
  • 累日不言去 - He did not mention leaving for several days.
  • 一夕正欢饮间 - One evening during their joyful drinking.
  • 云相公且至 - They said the governor was coming.
  • 夫人遽起 - The lady quickly got up.
  • 诸妾皆奔忙散 - The other concubines all ran around.
  • 钱窜伏暗室 - Qian hid in a dark room.
  • 不敢喘怖 - He dared not breathe or be afraid.
  • 因假寐久之 - He pretended to sleep for a long time.
  • 狐嘷鸦噪 - Foxes howled and crows cawed.
  • 东方既明 - The east became bright.
  • 人屋俱亡 - The people and houses had all disappeared.
  • 但已身卧于丛古冢耳 - He found himself lying in a thicket of ancient tombs.
  • 狼狈而出 - He came out in a state of distress.
  • 逢耕夫始得官道 - He met a farmer and finally found the official road.
  • 衣上余香芬馥 - The clothes still had a lingering fragrance.
  • 经月乃歇 - It lasted for a month before fading.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金皇统中 - The reign period of Emperor Shizong of Jin (Jin Huitong, 1149–1161).
  • 商虢 - Ancient regions in Shaanxi Province.
  • 鄠县 - An ancient county in Shaanxi Province.
  • 河中府尹 - The governor of Hedong Prefecture, a significant administrative region in northern China.
  • 张相公 - A high-ranking official, likely a minister or governor.
  • 明烛盈前 - Bright candles, indicating a well-lit and welcoming environment.
  • 夫人容色端妍 - Graceful and beautiful appearance, emphasizing the lady's elegance.
  • 冠服华盛 - Splendid attire, highlighting the lady's noble status.
  • 侍儿歌舞之妙 - Exquisite dancing and singing, showcasing the entertainment provided.
  • 钱自谓竒遇若游仙都 - A strange encounter like traveling to the fairy realm, reflecting Qian's awe and wonder.
  • 情思荡摇 - Stirred emotions, indicating a sense of excitement or confusion.
  • 莫知身世之所留 - Not knowing where one belongs, suggesting a sense of disorientation.
  • 拱手敬坐 - Standing respectfully, showing politeness and deference.
  • 不轻交一谈 - Not easily engaging in conversation, indicating shyness or reserve.
  • 诸人以为野戅 - Thought to be rustic and foolish, reflecting the others' judgment.
  • 相视笑侮 - Looking at each other and laughing mockingly, showing mockery and disdain.
  • 罢席就枕 - After the meal, going to bed, indicating the end of the evening.
  • 俄而烛至 - Soon after, the candles were lit again, suggesting a continuation of the evening.
  • 夫人者复来 - The lady came again, indicating her return.
  • 众拥之登床 - The others surrounded her and helped her onto the bed, showing their attentiveness.
  • 钱趋下辞避 - Qian hurriedly stepped down to avoid, indicating his reluctance.
  • 强之再三 - She insisted repeatedly, showing persistence.
  • 于是共寝 - They finally shared the bed, indicating a resolution.
  • 明旦留之饭 - The next morning, they kept him for breakfast, showing hospitality.
  • 钱本漂泊旅人 - Originally a wandering traveler, indicating Qian's background.
  • 既称惬怀抱 - Very satisfied, showing his contentment.
  • 累日不言去 - Did not mention leaving for several days, indicating his enjoyment.
  • 一夕正欢饮间 - One evening during their joyful drinking, showing a moment of happiness.
  • 云相公且至 - Said the governor was coming, showing the arrival of authority.
  • 夫人遽起 - The lady quickly got up, showing her urgency.
  • 诸妾皆奔忙散 - The other concubines all ran around, showing their panic.
  • 钱窜伏暗室 - Qian hid in a dark room.
  • 不敢喘怖 - He dared not breathe or be afraid.
  • 因假寐久之 - He pretended to sleep for a long time.
  • 狐嘷鸦噪 - Foxes howled and crows cawed.
  • 东方既明 - The east became bright.
  • 人屋俱亡 - The people and houses had all disappeared.
  • 但已身卧于丛古冢耳 - He found himself lying in a thicket of ancient tombs.
  • 狼狈而出 - He came out in a state of distress.
  • 逢耕夫始得官道 - He met a farmer and finally found the official road.
  • 衣上余香芬馥 - The clothes still had a lingering fragrance.
  • 经月乃歇 - It lasted for a month before fading.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 难言也 - Difficult to say; used when the villager is unable to explain his actions.
  • 获天佑 - Received divine protection; used to describe the villager's release from suspicion due to a miraculous event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岚州 - A prefecture in ancient China.
  • 宜芳县 - A county in ancient China.
  • 飞鸯堡 - A village in ancient China.
  • 楼烦县 - A county in ancient China.
  • 普光寺 - A temple in Wucheng, known for its historical significance.
  • 太守 - A high-ranking official in charge of a prefecture.
  • 保伍 - A system of local security and mutual responsibility.
  • 曹掾 - An official or assistant in the local government.
  • 疡医 - A specialist in treating wounds and skin conditions.
  • 街坊化士 - A wandering monk who travels and preaches.
  • 明生 - A specific official sent to investigate the case.
  • 孔彦璋 - A specific doctor who examines Yuan Hui.
  • 元晖 - A specific monk from Pu Guang Temple.

Proper Nouns

  • 岚州 - A prefecture in ancient China.
  • 宜芳县 - A county in ancient China.
  • 飞鸯堡 - A village in ancient China.
  • 楼烦县 - A county in ancient China.
  • 普光寺 - A temple in Wucheng, known for its historical significance.
  • 太守 - A high-ranking official in charge of a prefecture.
  • 保伍 - A system of local security and mutual responsibility.
  • 曹掾 - An official or assistant in the local government.

  • 街坊化士 - A wandering monk who travels and preaches.
  • 明生 - A specific official sent to investigate the case.
  • 孔彦璋 - A specific doctor who examines Yuan Hui.
  • 元晖 - A specific monk from Pu Guang Temple.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 日长尺许 - Metaphorical expression describing a gradual and significant increase, often used in fables or parables.
  • 遍体生毛 - Common motif in Chinese folklore and literature, symbolizing a transformation or curse.
  • 首面已肖驴形 - Specific description of a transformation, often used in stories of shape-shifting or curses.
  • 两耳翘翘然 - Detailed description of the transformed individual's appearance, emphasizing the completeness of the transformation.
  • 哮吼悲鸣 - Description of the transformed individual's behavior, conveying fear or distress.
  • 四支据地卓立 - Description of the transformed individual's posture, emphasizing the physical changes.
  • 俨成真驴 - Final description of the transformed individual, confirming the transformation.
  • 背天理 - Concept in Chinese philosophy and religion, emphasizing adherence to divine laws.
  • 弗施辔勒 - Description of the lack of control, emphasizing the wild nature of the transformed individual.
  • 驴嘶噉不已 - Description of the transformed individual's behavior, conveying the intensity of the transformation.
  • 乱齧人 - Description of the aggressive behavior, conveying the danger posed by the transformed individual.
  • 试举鞍置前 - Attempt to control the transformed individual, showing efforts to manage the situation.
  • 耸耳以待 - Description of the transformed individual's reaction, conveying a sense of anticipation or curiosity.
  • 若有喜意 - Description of the transformed individual's emotional state, conveying a sense of joy or contentment.
  • 重致远 - Description of the transformed individual's capabilities, emphasizing strength and endurance.
  • 能日行二百里 - Description of the transformed individual's speed, conveying extraordinary abilities.
  • 十年方死 - Description of the lifespan, emphasizing the duration of the transformation.
  • 累月不雨 - Description of a prolonged drought, setting the scene for a story involving natural disasters.
  • 祷于岳岱 - Reference to Mount Tai, a sacred mountain in Chinese culture, often used in religious rituals.
  • 不应 - Description of the lack of response, conveying disappointment or frustration.
  • 继致祭龙潭 - Reference to the Dragon Pond, often used in stories involving water and divine intervention.
  • 赫日滋炽 - Description of the worsening of the drought, conveying the severity of the situation.
  • 天久无雨 - Description of the prolonged drought, setting the scene for a story involving divine retribution.
  • 四海之内 - Reference to the entire world, often used in stories involving universal themes.
  • 一勺之水 - Metaphorical expression describing even a small amount of water, often used in stories involving scarcity.
  • 神主之 - Reference to the presence of divine control, often used in stories involving supernatural elements.
  • 吾弗得预 - Description of the inability of the spirits to intervene, conveying a sense of helplessness.
  • 天律 - Concept in Chinese philosophy and religion, emphasizing divine rules.
  • 降膏泽 - Description of the act of bringing rain, often used to convey divine intervention.
  • 察之也 - Description of the need for understanding, often used to convey a moral lesson.
  • 愈怒 - Description of an increase in anger, often used to convey emotional intensity.
  • 率千余卒 - Description of the mobilization of troops, often used to convey military action.
  • 益辇石土 - Description of the intensification of efforts, often used to convey determination.
  • 投寘潭内 - Description of the act of filling the pond, often used to convey the completion of a task.
  • 比暮遂平 - Description of the completion of the filling, often used to convey the resolution of a problem.
  • 复如故 - Description of the restoration of the pond, often used to convey a return to normalcy.
  • 了不警悟 - Description of a lack of realization, often used to convey ignorance or obliviousness.
  • 竭人力而为之 - Description of the intense effort, often used to convey the magnitude of human endeavor.
  • 隆寒风 - Description of the sudden change in weather, often used to convey the unpredictability of nature.
  • 雷电从潭出 - Description of a natural phenomenon, often used to convey divine retribution or supernatural events.
  • 山阜皆震 - Description of the intensity of the event, often used to convey the power of nature.
  • 惧甚 - Description of the fear of the people, often used to convey the impact of natural disasters.
  • 督役不已 - Description of the continuous supervision, often used to convey the persistence of human effort.
  • 暴卒 - Description of the unexpected death, often used to convey the unpredictability of life.
  • 沛然 - Description of the sudden arrival of rain, often used to convey relief or divine intervention.

  • 视王而笑 - Means 'looking at Wang and smiling,' indicating a non-verbal interaction.
  • 王平生山行野宿习见物怪 - Means 'Wang, who was accustomed to living in the mountains and forests, had seen many strange creatures,' suggesting his familiarity with the supernatural.
  • 虽知为非人殊无惧色 - Means 'although he knew she was not human, he showed no fear,' indicating his calm demeanor.

  • 良久乃问王曰尔何人 - Means 'after a long time, she asked Wang, 'Who are you?'' showing her eventual curiosity.
  • 王始稍敬异揖而言 - Means 'Wang then slightly bowed and spoke respectfully,' indicating a change in his attitude.
  • 本山下猎徒 - Means 'a hunter from the mountains,' reinforcing his identity.
  • 今日逐虎失踪 - Means 'today while chasing a tiger, I got lost,' explaining his current situation.
  • 生死之分只在须顷 - Means 'life and death are just a moment apart,' emphasizing the urgency of his situation.
  • 愿娘子哀之 - Means 'I beg you to pity me,' showing his plea for help.

  • 涂径荦确足力不能给 - Means 'the path was rough, and his feet could not keep up,' highlighting the physical strain.
  • 女不穿屦步武如飞 - Means 'the woman did not wear shoes, but walked as fast as flying,' emphasizing her supernatural abilities.
  • 到一洞有大石室 - Means 'they arrived at a cave with a large stone room,' describing the destination.
  • 境趣防寂如幽人居 - Means 'the place was quiet and secluded, like a hermit's abode,' indicating the peaceful atmosphere.
  • 寝室尤洁雅 - Means 'the bedroom was especially clean and elegant,' describing the living conditions.
  • 王顾傍无他人戏言挑之 - Means 'Wang, seeing no one else around, teased her,' showing his playful nature.
  • 欣然相就 - Means 'she happily accepted,' indicating her positive response.
  • 夜则共榻昼则出求果实以啖之 - Means 'at night they shared a bed, and during the day she went out to find fruits to eat,' describing their daily routine.
  • 居月余 - Means 'they lived there for more than a month,' indicating the duration of their stay.
  • 王念母乏供养以情泣告 - Means 'Wang thought of his mother's lack of support and wept, telling her,' showing his concern for his family.
  • 女曰我欲暂归徐当复相寻 - Means 'the woman said, 'I want to return briefly, and we will meet again soon,''' indicating her willingness to help.
  • 女许诺送出官道乃别 - Means 'the woman agreed and escorted him to the official road before parting,' showing her kindness.
  • 王感其意爱他日再访焉 - Means 'Wang was moved by her kindness and decided to visit her again one day,' indicating his gratitude.
  • 试与之语邀同归 - Means 'he tried talking to her and invited her to return home,' showing his desire for a deeper connection.
  • 略不谦拒 - Means 'she did not refuse modestly,' indicating her openness.
  • 携手抵家 - Means 'they held hands and reached home,' describing their arrival.
  • 王妻赵氏既育三男女矣 - Means 'Wang's wife Zhao had already given birth to three children,' providing background on his family.
  • 此女又生两子 - Means 'this woman gave birth to two more children,' indicating the addition to the family.
  • 与赵共处甚雍睦 - Means 'they lived together very harmoniously with Zhao,' describing the family dynamics.

  • 小説载释照讲法华经于嵩山有三叟日来谛听自言是龙照以天旱令降雨叟曰雨禁絶重傥不奉命擅行诟责非细唯孙处士能解弟子之祸照为谒孙思邈致恳是夜千里雨足三叟化为獭匿于孙所居后沼遭使者捕执孙使解而释之事颇相 - A story tells of Master Xuanzhao preaching the Lotus Sutra on Mount Song, three old men came daily to listen and said they were dragons, Xuanzhao, seeing a drought, commanded the rain to fall, the old men said, 'The rain is forbidden; if we act without permission, we will be severely reprimanded,' only Master Sun, a recluse, can resolve our disciples' troubles, Xuanzhao went to see Sun Simiao and pleaded with him, that night, it rained for a thousand miles, the three old men transformed into otters and hid in the pond behind Sun's residence, they were caught by messengers, but Sun intervened and released them, this event is somewhat similar to others.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 项明取倡女胡氏为妻 - 取 in this context means 'to marry,' so '项明娶倡女胡氏为妻' is more idiomatic in English.
  • 十有余岁 - Literally 'ten plus years,' which can be rendered as 'over ten years.'
  • 生一女而死 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'she gave birth to a daughter and then died' or 'she gave birth to a daughter who then died.' Context suggests the former.
  • 隆兴元年 - The first year of the Longxing era, which was 1163 CE.
  • 有巫从于他乡来言能致亡者魂魄 - 有巫 means 'there was a witch' or 'a witch appeared,' and 从于他乡来 means 'came from another place.' 言能致亡者魂魄 means 'said she could summon the soul of the deceased.'
  • 项令召其妻随命即至 - 项令 means 'Xiang ordered,' and 召其妻 means 'his wife.' 随命即至 means 'upon command, she immediately arrived.'
  • 项无所覩女已二十岁 - 项无所覩 means 'Xiang saw nothing,' and 女已二十岁 means 'the daughter was already twenty years old.'
  • 独见之真其母也 - 独见之 means 'only saw,' and 真其母也 means 'she was indeed her mother.'
  • 遂留止不去夜与夫同榻异衾 - 遂留止不去 means 'she stayed and did not leave,' and 夜与夫同榻异衾 means 'living at night with her husband on the same bed but with different quilts.'
  • 凡所需索悉凭女以告 - 凡所需索 means 'whatever was needed,' and 悉凭女以告 means 'all was relied on the daughter to inform.'
  • 两月后忽云父母来 - 两月后忽云 means 'after two months, she suddenly said,' and 父母来 means 'parents came.'
  • 仍携仆从欲饮食项即办供具 - 仍携仆从 means 'still with attendants,' and 欲饮食项即办供具 means 'they wanted to eat, and Xiang immediately prepared food.'
  • 初同席铺设妻曰主仆不当均礼乃别置焉 - 初同席铺设 means 'at first, they shared the same table and seating,' and 妻曰主仆不当均礼乃别置焉 means 'the wife said, 'The master and servant should not be treated equally,' and then placed them separately.'
  • 继言吾父室庐损敝拟造新居求钱助费亟焚纸镪数百束 - 继言吾父室庐损敝 means 'then she said, 'My father's house is damaged,' and 拟造新居求钱助费亟焚纸镪数百束 means 'we plan to build a new house. We need money for expenses. Quickly burned hundreds of bundles of paper money.'
  • 钱甚多无人辇送 - 钱甚多 means 'very much,' and 无人辇送 means 'no one to carry and deliver.'
  • 乃唤画工作两力人 - 乃唤画工作两力人 means 'then called two laborers.'
  • 既成嫌其矬弱复易之 - 既成嫌其矬弱 means 'after completion, found them weak,' and 复易之 means 'replaced them.'
  • 俄告去 - 俄告去 means 'soon announced to leave.'
  • 欲偕二亲治屋 - 欲偕二亲治屋 means 'want to build a house with two parents.'
  • 经月余再来 - 经月余再来 means 'after more than a month, came back.'
  • 所亲李媪访其夫 - 所亲李媪访其夫 means 'a relative, Old Lady Li, visited her husband.'
  • 夫不言 - 夫不言 means 'husband did not speak.'
  • 问女云已被阿娘于腰间打一杵 - 问女云已被阿娘于腰间打一杵 means 'asked the daughter, who said she had already been hit by her mother on the waist with a stick.'
  • 媪归腰大痛不能行 - 媪归腰大痛不能行 means 'Old Lady Li returned, had severe back pain, and could not walk.'
  • 卜者占胡氏为祟 - 卜者占胡氏为祟 means 'diviner divined that Hu was causing trouble.'
  • 祷之始愈 - 祷之始愈 means 'prayed to her, began to recover.'
  • 相处一年渐缩小 - 相处一年渐缩小 means 'lived together for one year, gradually shrank.'
  • 殆如婴儿 - 殆如婴儿 means 'almost like a baby.'
  • 遂不见 - 遂不见 means 'then disappeared.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 游节妇 - Title referring to a woman named You known for her chastity and virtue.
  • 建昌南城近郭南原村民六素蠢朴一意农圃 - Describes a simple, uneducated farmer named Pang Liu from the outskirts of Nancheng, Jianchang. '蠢朴' suggests he is naive and unsophisticated, while '一意农圃' indicates his sole focus on farming.
  • 其弟妇游氏在侪辈中稍泽悍戾淫泆与并舍少年奸每侧目唾骂无如之何 - Describes You's sister-in-law, who is described as 'slightly beautiful' but also 'violent and promiscuous.' She engages in adultery with young men from the same village, which leads to Pang Liu being constantly insulted and spat upon, yet he is powerless to stop it.
  • 游尝攘鸡欲烹知之入其房捜索得鸡以出游遽以刃自伤手走至邻舍大呼曰伯以吾夫不在家持只鸡为饵强胁污我我不肯从怀刀见杀幸而得免适无妻邻人以为然执诣里正赴县狱 - Describes an incident where You steals a chicken and prepares to cook it. Pang Liu discovers this and searches her room, finding the chicken. He then attacks her with a knife, causing her to injure herself. She flees to a neighbor's house and falsely accuses Pang Liu of trying to rape her, claiming he used a chicken as bait. The neighbors believe her and take her to the village head, who sends her to the county jail.
  • 狱吏审其情实需钱十千将为作道地贫而啬且自恃理直坚不许吏傅防成案上于军军守戴顗不能察且闾阎匹妇而能守义保身不受凌偪录事参军赵师景又迎合顗意锻链成狱奏之坐死而赐游氏钱十万令长吏岁时存问以旌其节由是有节妇之称郡人尽知寃而愤游之滥竟以与比近林田寺僧通为人所告受杖未几抱疾见为祟遂死时淳熙四年六月也其后顗为提防刑狱延玺劾罢赵赃败去官军县推吏一死一黥皆相去年岁间耳 - Describes the legal proceedings and the subsequent events. The county magistrate finds that You needed money to buy a plot of land, and Pang Liu, being poor and stingy, refused to give it to her. The magistrate, unable to see the truth, accepts the false accusation. Scribe Judge Zhao Jing supports the magistrate's decision, and Pang Liu is sentenced to death. However, You receives ten thousand coins as a reward, and the magistrate orders officials to visit her annually to honor her virtue. This leads to the title '贞节妇' (chaste and virtuous woman), which becomes widely known in the county. However, Pang Liu is later found innocent, and the officials who wronged him are punished.

  • 常怪乐天长恨词 - Refers to Bai Juyi's (White居易) famous poem 'The Song of Everlasting Sorrow,' which laments the tragic love story of Emperor Xuanzong and Yang Guifei.
  • 钗钿寄语太伤悲 - Refers to the emotional weight of sending jewelry or personal items as a message, symbolizing deep sorrow or regret.
  • 蓬山上有忆人间音问时 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'in the celestial realm, there is a longing for human voices and messages,' suggesting a yearning for connection with the earthly world.
  • 玉女倚天多喜笑 - Describes a beautiful woman (often a goddess) leaning against the sky, smiling, which conveys a sense of grace and joy.
  • 素娥如月与精神 - Refers to a pale, ethereal woman (often the Moon Goddess Chang'e) who embodies the spirit of the moon, symbolizing purity and beauty.
  • 假饶不许长年住 - A conditional phrase meaning 'even if one is not allowed to stay for long,' suggesting a temporary or fleeting nature.

  • 琼浆饮罢日西沈 - Refers to drinking a divine elixir (琼浆) and watching the sun set, symbolizing the passage of time and the transition from day to night.
  • 瞬息欢游直万金 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'a moment of joy is worth more than gold,' emphasizing the value of fleeting happiness.
  • 尘虑满那住得 - A phrase meaning 'how can one stay when worldly concerns fill the mind?' suggesting the impossibility of remaining in a state of peace or detachment.
  • 鳯箫休作别离音 - Refers to the sound of a phoenix flute (鳯箫), which is often associated with farewell or parting, suggesting that the music should not evoke sadness.

  • 曲终而寤箫声故在耳后两夕复梦童携诗牌白曰仙子谢君玉女即天女也 - A complex sentence describing a dream where the woman wakes up to hear the flute, then dreams again of a child carrying a poem plaque, explaining that the '仙子' (immortal) is actually a celestial maiden.
  • 素蛾月精以见况甚无谓刘阮太真列仙也常相往还君何訾诋之甚 - A statement criticizing the woman for belittling the immortals, comparing her to Liu An and Ruan Zhou, and asking why she would insult them.
  • 老子为九天最尊奈何辄斥其名今为易老耼二字为道家仙格劣三字为苦轻肆皆凡猥三字为那真实陈悉依其语童遂去 - A passage where the woman is instructed to replace '老子' (Laozi) with '道家仙格劣' (a lesser Taoist deity) and '轻肆皆凡猥' (a derogatory term for the ordinary), and she follows the child's instructions.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is a complex poetic description that must be read as a single unit, describing a mythical setting and the confusion of Liu An and Ruan Zhou.
  • This sentence is a series of actions and events that must be read as a sequence: the woman writes, plays a flute, wakes up, and then dreams again.
  • This sentence is a rhetorical question criticizing the woman for belittling the immortals, comparing her to Liu An and Ruan Zhou.
  • This sentence is a series of instructions and actions: the woman is told to replace '老子' with '道家仙格劣' and '轻肆皆凡猥,' and she follows the child's instructions.
  • This sentence is a series of actions and a rhetorical question: the woman is described as a frivolous scholar who laughs and leaves, and the narrator writes about her, comparing her to the celestial maidens of Tang dynasty legends.
  • This sentence is a complex description of Huang Ze, his dream, and the setting.
  • This sentence is a continuation of the dream, where Huang Ze is upset, and later Zhang Cheng marries Huang Ze's wife after both are widowed.

  • 正昼熟寝父母忧之父随其所往杂居众中伺察见女来就福绸缪欢聚 - During the day, he would sleep deeply, and his parents were worried. His father followed him wherever he went, mingling with the crowd to spy on him, and saw the woman coming to meet Wang Fu, they were intimately happy together.
  • 明日呼扣之且问其病不肯言但云我未尝有病父怒欲施杖责方以实吿父为谒假使在家治疗又宻询郡舍老兵果有妳婆一女讶其安得常常出外且信且疑他日因福再上直复诣彼审视女又来父持灯逐捕女狼狈起走入天王祠而没明旦验之盖捧装奁侍女也引福至前低首不语于是击碎其像福掩面嗟惜堕泪逾旬而死 - The next day, his father called him and scolded him, asking about his illness, but he refused to say anything, only saying he had never been sick. His father became angry and wanted to beat him, but Wang Fu finally told the truth. His father arranged for him to stay home for treatment and secretly asked the old soldiers in the prefecture if there was indeed an old woman's daughter, and they confirmed it. They were surprised that she could often go out, and they were both surprised and doubtful. One day, when Wang Fu was on duty again, his father went there to investigate, and the woman came again. His father lit a lamp and chased her, and she ran in a panic into the Temple of the Heavenly King and disappeared. The next morning, they checked and found that she was a servant girl holding a bridal chest. They brought Wang Fu forward, and he lowered his head and said nothing. His father then smashed the image of the Heavenly King. Wang Fu covered his face, sighed, and cried, and died after more than ten days.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 羅維藩字價卿 - Lu Weifan, courtesy name Jiaqing. In Classical Chinese, the surname comes first, followed by the given name and then the courtesy name.
  • 乾道五年省試 - The provincial examination in the fifth year of the Qian Dao era. '乾道' (Qian Dao) is an era name of the Song Dynasty, and '省試' refers to the provincial-level imperial examination.
  • 夢其父吿曰 - Dreamed that his father told him. This is a common narrative device in Classical Chinese to convey divine or ancestral guidance.
  • 爾在舉場不可與福唐杜申爭 - You should not compete with Fu Tang Du Shen in the examination hall. This is a direct instruction, likely based on a premonition or dream.
  • 爾家校杜申亏了三十八年陰徳也 - Your family has owed Du Shen thirty-eight years of merit. '陰徳' (yin de) refers to hidden or unseen virtue, often associated with good deeds done without seeking recognition.
  • 兩人皆以治詩有聲 - Both of you are famous for studying the Book of Poetry. '治詩' means 'to study the Book of Poetry,' and '有聲' means 'to be famous or renowned.'
  • 暨榜出杜為經魁羅同奏籍而在杜之下二十八名 - When the results were announced, Du was the top scorer in the Confucian classics, while Luo was listed twenty-eighth below Du. '經魁' refers to the top scorer in the Confucian classics section of the exam.
  • 殿廷唱第杜居第二甲羅第四甲 - In the imperial court, Du was ranked second in the second class, while Luo was ranked fourth in the fourth class. '殿廷' refers to the imperial court, and '唱第' means 'to announce the rankings.'
  • 夷堅志甲卷七 - The seventh volume of the Yi Jian Zhi, Class A. '夷堅志' (Yi Jian Zhi) is a collection of strange stories and legends compiled by Hong Mai during the Song Dynasty.
  • 欽定四庫全書 - Authorized by the imperial decree, the Four Treasuries. '欽定' means 'authorized by the emperor,' and '四庫全書' is a massive compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing Dynasty.
  • 戴之邵夢 - The dream of Dai Zhi Shao. This is the title of a section in the Yi Jian Zhi, likely recounting a dream experience.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '公居彼愿见无因稍扫人之门以希一盼' is elliptical and can be read as 'the official had no reason to see him, so he tidied up the official's door in hopes of a glance.'
  • The sentence '值其诞日夙造听事以红粉书寿字于地广长二丈许' is a long sequence of actions, describing the person arriving early at the official's office on his birthday and writing the character for 'longevity' on the ground with red powder.
  • The sentence '公出见问为谁隶以戴道人对' is a sequence of actions, describing the official asking who the person was and the person responding with the name 'Dai Dao Ren.'
  • The sentence '命呼至前犒以缗钱尊酒辞不受曰之邵妄意功名所望相公者固不在此辄衒竒以自售' is a long sequence of actions, describing the official ordering the person to be called forward and rewarding them with money and wine, but the person declined to accept, saying that 'Zhi Shao' had wrong intentions, aspired to fame and success, and certainly not here, always showed off one's talents to sell oneself.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以担为业 - Literally 'to take carrying (a load) as one's profession,' meaning to be a porter or laborer who carries goods on a pole or shoulder pole. The character  is likely a variant or corrupted form of '擔' (dān), meaning 'to carry.'
  • 在辈稍光泽 - 'Among her peers, somewhat attractive' or 'slightly more attractive than others.' 光泽 (guāngzé) usually means 'luster' or 'radiance,' here used metaphorically for beauty.
  • 语言挑捷 - 'Words were smooth and persuasive,' or 'fluent and seductive in speech.' 挑捷 (tiǎojié) implies quick-wittedness and persuasive speech.
  • 谑欲与奸 - 'Teased and wished to commit adultery.' 谑 (xuè) means 'tease' or 'jest,' while 奸 (jiān) means 'adultery' or 'immoral conduct.'
  • 袖出白金数两 - 'Pulled out several taels of silver from his sleeve.' A common expression for secretly offering money.
  • 荏苒颇久 - 'A long time passed.' 荏苒 (rěnrǎn) is a literary term for the passage of time.
  • 诈与妻曰 - 'Lied to his wife, saying...' 诈 (zhà) means 'to lie' or 'deceive.'
  • 旬日乃可囘 - 'Ten days and I will return.' 旬日 (xún rì) is a classical term for ten days.
  • 潜反室 - 'Slipped back into the room.' 潜 (qián) means 'to sneak' or 'to hide.'

  • 夜且二鼓 - 'It was nearly the second drum (i.e., about 10 PM).' 二鼓 (èr gǔ) refers to the second watch of the night in ancient Chinese timekeeping.
  • 从槛越入 - 'Climbed over the window frame to enter.' 楷 (jiàn) is a window frame or lattice.
  • 迎刺以矛 - 'Struck with a spear.' 迎刺 (yíng cì) means 'to attack head-on.'
  • 呦呦作声 - 'Made a sound like 'yōuyōu.' 呦呦 (yōuyōu) is an onomatopoeic term often used for the sound of a bird or a person gasping.
  • 细毛数十茎 - 'Dozens of fine hairs.' 茎 (jīng) here refers to strands or hairs.
  • 人安得有毛 - 'How could a person have hair?' 安得 (ān dé) is a rhetorical question meaning 'how could it be that...'
  • 此必恠也 - 'This must be a supernatural being.' 恠 (guài) means 'strange' or 'supernatural.'
  • 不复穷诘 - 'Did not pursue further questioning.' 穷诘 (qióng jié) means 'to exhaustively question' or 'to investigate thoroughly.'
  • 始肻言所见 - 'Finally admitted what she saw.' 肯 (kěn) means 'to consent' or 'to finally agree to speak.'
  • 具一牒述始末 - 'Wrote a formal document detailing the beginning and end.' 牒 (dié) is a formal written statement or report.
  • 如供状式 - 'In the style of a confession.' 供状 (gòngzhuàng) is a legal term for a written confession.
  • 诣道士混元法师堇中甫 - 'Visited the Daoist master, Master Hunyuan Jin Zhongfu.' 混元 (hùnyuán) is a Daoist term meaning 'the primordial whole' or 'the ultimate.' 法师 (fǎshī) is 'Daoist master' or 'sage.' 堇中甫 (Jin Zhongfu) is the name of the Daoist.
  • 依科作罩法 - 'Performed a protective ritual according to the established rites.' 罩法 (zhào fǎ) is a type of Daoist protective or binding ritual.
  • 发符拱立以俟 - 'Unrolled the talisman and stood in a respectful posture, waiting.' 发符 (fā fú) is to unroll or release a talisman; 拱立 (gǒng lì) is to stand with hands clasped in respect.
  • 少还有大鹰盘空 - 'After a while, a large eagle circled in the sky.' 盘空 (pán kōng) means 'to circle in the sky.'
  • 观者阗溢 - 'The onlookers were packed.' 阗溢 (tián yì) means 'full to the brim.'
  • 径搏巨白鼠 - 'Struck a large white rat directly.' 搏 (bó) means 'to strike' or 'to wrestle.'
  • 衔掷于前 - 'Carried it and threw it forward.' 衔 (xián) means 'to carry in the mouth'; 掷 (zhì) means 'to throw.'

  • 兄曰然则知我至则绝迹何邪 - Brother's question about the spirits' behavior, which can be read as a single unit.

Together, it means that Huang arrived at a lodging place late in the evening after rushing through his journey.
  • 行商走卒充满其间 - Means 'merchants and foot soldiers filled the space,' indicating a crowded and bustling environment.

  • 主者不得辞 - Means 'the host could not refuse,' indicating that the innkeeper had no choice but to accommodate Huang.
  • 旅空一室与之处 - Means 'the host provided an empty room for Huang to stay in,' showing the innkeeper's accommodation.
  • 翁夜梦黄龙从外至以爪抉门入蟠踞中堂光焰赫奕惊而寤 - This is a vivid and complex dream description. '翁' refers to the innkeeper, '夜梦' means 'had a dream at night,' '黄龙' means 'yellow dragon,' '从外至' means 'came from outside,' '以爪抉门入' means 'used its claws to pry open the door,' '蟠踞中堂' means 'coiled and settled in the main hall,' '光焰赫奕' means 'with brilliant flames,' and '惊而寤' means 'woke up in alarm.' Together, it describes the innkeeper's dream of a yellow dragon entering the main hall.
  • 疑必有贵客在店未明起访焉 - This phrase is somewhat elliptical. '疑必有贵客在店' means 'suspected that there must be a distinguished guest in the inn,' and '未明起访焉' means 'got up before dawn to visit.' Together, it means the innkeeper suspected a distinguished guest and visited early in the morning.
  • 歴视十数客皆不足当独黄君为士子意其兆应是也 - This phrase is also somewhat elliptical. '歴视十数客' means 'visited several guests,' '皆不足当' means 'none were worthy,' and '独黄君为士子意其兆应是也' means 'only Huang, a scholar, seemed to be the one who matched the omen.' Together, it means the innkeeper visited several guests but found only Huang to be worthy.
  • 又问知姓黄益自信戒仆俟盥栉毕茶汤诣之为话宵梦乞志之于壁 - This phrase is complex. '又问知姓黄' means 'asked and learned that the guest's surname was Huang,' '益自信' means 'became more confident,' '戒仆俟盥栉毕' means 'ordered the servant to wait until Huang finished washing,' '茶汤诣之' means 'to bring tea and soup to him,' and '为话宵梦乞志之于壁' means 'to discuss the dream and ask him to write it on the wall.' Together, it means the innkeeper became more confident, ordered the servant to wait, and brought tea and soup to Huang to discuss the dream and ask him to write it on the wall.
  • 黄固心喜然亦不敢率尔力拒欲弗聴翁退复遣二儿来皆儒衣冠度不可已乃勉书 - This phrase is also complex. '黄固心喜' means 'Huang was secretly pleased,' '然亦不敢率尔' means 'but dared not act rashly,' '力拒欲弗聴' means 'to strongly refuse and not listen,' '翁退复遣二儿来' means 'the innkeeper sent his two sons again,' '皆儒衣冠' means 'all dressed in scholar's attire,' '度不可已' means 'thought it could not be avoided,' and '乃勉书' means 'and reluctantly wrote.' Together, it means Huang was pleased but dared not refuse, and the innkeeper sent his sons again, dressed in scholar's attire, to persuade Huang, who then reluctantly wrote the dream on the wall.

  • 恐苍卒沾濡无以自蔽 - He feared that he would be soaked and have no way to protect himself. '恐' (kǒng) means to fear, '苍卒' (cāng zú) means suddenly, '沾濡' (zhān rú) means to be soaked, and '无以自蔽' (wú yǐ zì bì) means to have no way to protect oneself.

  • 尝游行到岳识其人 - He once traveled to the mountains and recognized the person. '尝' (cháng) means once, '游行' (yóu xíng) means to travel, '到岳' (dào yuè) means to the mountains, and '识其人' (shí qí rén) means to recognize the person.

  • 孔庙释奠时跻配享而封舒王 - Refers to the ceremony of offering sacrifices to Confucius at the Confucian temple and the elevation to the rank of co-offering and enfeoffment as the Prince of Shu.
  • 优人设孔子正坐顔孟与安石侍侧 - Describes a performance where Confucius is seated centrally, and Yan Hui, Mencius, and Wang Anshi are on either side.

  • 位地有间辞之过矣 - Yan Hui acknowledges the difference in status and suggests that declining is excessive.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 议欲升安石于孟子之右为此而止 - Intention to elevate Wang Anshi above Mencius for this reason, and that was all.
  • 尝设三辈为儒道释各称诵其教 - Once set up three groups representing Confucianism, Taoism, and Buddhism, each reciting their teachings.
  • 吾之所学仁义礼智信曰五常 - What I have learned is benevolence, righteousness, propriety, wisdom, and faithfulness, which are called the Five Constants.
  • 遂演畅其防皆采引经书不杂媟语 - Thus, they elaborated on their defenses, all citing classical texts without mixing in vulgar language.
  • 次至道士曰吾之所学金木水火土曰五行 - Next, the Taoist said, 'What I have learned is metal, wood, water, fire, and earth, which are called the Five Elements.'
  • 亦说大意末至僧僧抵掌曰二子腐生常谈不足聴 - Also speaking the general idea, when it came to the monk, he clapped his hands and said, 'You two are just talking about stale, common knowledge.'
  • 吾之所学生老病死苦曰五化藏经渊奥非汝等所得闻 - What I have learned is birth, old age, illness, death, and suffering, which are called the Five Transformations. The scriptures are profound and not something you can understand.
  • 当以观世佛菩萨法理之妙为汝陈之盍以次问我 - I will explain the wonderful principles of the Buddha and Bodhisattvas to you. Why don't you ask me in order?
  • 敢问生曰内自大学辟雍外至下州偏县凡秀才读书者尽为三舎生 - I dare to ask about birth, saying that from the inner university to the outer provinces, all scholars who read are all three-shed students.
  • 华屋美月书季考三嵗大比脱白挂緑上可以为卿相国家之于生也如此 - They live in beautiful houses, read monthly books, and are tested quarterly. After three years, they are compared, and if they pass, they can become high officials. The state treats scholars like this.
  • 敢问老曰孤独贫困必沦沟壑今所在立孤老院养之终身国家之于老也如此 - I dare to ask about old age, saying that if one is lonely, poor, and in need, they will end up in the ditches. Now, there are orphan and elderly homes where they are cared for their entire lives. The state treats the elderly like this.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 安石 - Wang Anshi, a prominent statesman and scholar of the Song Dynasty.
  • 孟子 - Mencius, a Confucian philosopher and one of the most influential figures in Chinese thought.
  • 儒道释 - Confucianism, Taoism, and Buddhism, the three major philosophical and religious traditions in Chinese history.
  • 五常 - The Five Constants: benevolence, righteousness, propriety, wisdom, and faithfulness, which are central to Confucian ethics.
  • 五行 - The Five Elements: metal, wood, water, fire, and earth, which are central to Taoist cosmology.
  • 五化 - The Five Transformations: birth, old age, illness, death, and suffering, which are central to Buddhist teachings.
  • 大学辟雍 - The Imperial Academy, the highest institution of learning in ancient China.
  • 三舎生 - Students in the three-shed system, a hierarchical educational structure in ancient China.
  • 卿相 - High-ranking officials in the imperial court.
  • 孤老院 - Orphan and elderly homes, institutions for the care of orphans and the elderly.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentences are elliptical and must be read as single statements about the intention to elevate Wang Anshi above Mencius, setting up three groups representing Confucianism, Taoism, and Buddhism, what the speaker has learned and the Five Constants, elaborating on their defenses and citing classical texts, the Taoist's teachings and the Five Elements, the monk's reaction to the previous teachings, the monk's teachings and the Five Transformations, explaining the wonderful principles of the Buddha and Bodhisattvas, asking about birth and the three-shed system, the state's treatment of scholars, the state's treatment of the elderly, the state's treatment of the sick, the state's treatment of the dead, the state's treatment of the suffering, and nothing being unhelpful in matters.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 問病曰 - Formulaic opening to a dialogue, meaning 'When asked about illness, it was said...'
  • 不幸而有病家貧不能拯療 - Compound phrase meaning 'unluckily having illness, and the family poor, unable to seek treatment.'
  • 安濟坊 - Classical institution for medical care, often government-run, meaning 'Peace and Rescue Hall' or 'Public Hospital.'
  • 使之存處差醫付藥責以十全之効 - Complex clause meaning 'they are given shelter, assigned doctors, given medicine, and held responsible for full recovery.'
  • 其於病也如此 - Concluding phrase meaning 'this is how it is treated in the case of illness.'
  • 敢問死 - Formulaic question meaning 'Dare I ask about death?'
  • 死者人所不免 - Common expression meaning 'death is something no one can escape.'
  • 無所歸 - 'Nowhere to return' or 'no place to be buried.'
  • 漏澤園 - Classical term for public burial grounds for the poor, meaning 'Dripping Moisture Garden' or 'Public Cemetery.'
  • 無以斂 - 'Unable to afford burial.'
  • 與之棺使得葬埋 - 'They are given coffins so that they may be buried.'
  • 春秋享祀 - Refers to seasonal offerings and rituals, meaning 'seasonal sacrifices and rites.'
  • 恩及泉壤 - Poetic expression meaning 'mercy extends even to the underworld' or 'kindness reaches the grave.'
  • 其於死也如此 - 'This is how it is treated in the case of death.'
  • 敢問苦 - 'Dare I ask about suffering?'
  • 瞑目不应 - 'Closed eyes, unresponsive' – describing someone in deep suffering or death.
  • 陽恻悚然 - Compound term meaning 'the person is visibly moved with compassion and alarm.'
  • 促之再三 - 'Urged repeatedly.'
  • 蹙額答曰 - 'Furrowed brow in reply' – a gesture of hesitation or distress.
  • 只是百姓一般受無量苦 - 'It is simply that the common people suffer endlessly.'
  • 徽宗為恻然長思弗以為罪 - 'Emperor Huizong was moved with compassion and reflected long, not holding it as a crime.'
  • 绍兴中 - Refers to the middle period of the Song dynasty during the reign of Emperor Gaozong (Shaoxing era).
  • 李椿年行經界量田法 - Reference to the implementation of the 'jingjie liangtian' (boundary and land measurement) system by Li Chunnian.
  • 方事之初郡邑奉命嚴急 - 'At the beginning of the project, the prefectures and counties received strict orders.'
  • 民當其職者 - 'Those who held the office or responsibility.'
  • 困苦之 - 'Were made to suffer.'
  • 优者为先圣先师鼎足而坐 - Metaphor for the most capable individuals sitting in the positions of the sages and teachers, like the three legs of a tripod.
  • 弟子从末席起咨叩所疑 - 'Disciples from the lowest seat arose to ask questions.'
  • 孟子奋曰 - 'Mencius rose and said.'
  • 仁政必自经界始 - Direct quote from Mencius, meaning 'Benevolent governance must begin with land boundaries.'
  • 吾下世千五百年其言乃为圣世所施用 - 'I have been dead for fifteen centuries, yet my words are now used in this saintly age.'
  • 三千之徒皆不如我 - 'All my three thousand disciples are not as good as I am.'
  • 顔子黙 - 'Yan Hui was silent' – a reference to Mencius quoting or alluding to a passage from the Analects of Confucius.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 安濟坊 - State-run medical institution established during the Song dynasty to provide care for the poor and sick.
  • 漏澤園 - Public cemeteries for the poor, established to provide burial rights to those who could not afford them.
  • 徽宗 - Emperor Huizong of Song (r. 1100–1125), known for his artistic patronage but also for his weak rule and the fall of the Northern Song.
  • 绍兴中 - The Shaoxing era (1131–1162) of the Southern Song dynasty, during the reign of Emperor Gaozong.
  • 李椿年 - A Song dynasty official known for implementing land reform and boundary surveys.
  • 經界量田法 - A land survey and boundary-delineation system used to assess land for taxation and to prevent land consolidation.
  • 先圣先师 - Refers to Confucius and other ancient sages and teachers.
  • 鼎足而坐 - Metaphor for the three legs of a tripod, symbolizing the three most capable people in a group.
  • 孟子奋曰 - Reference to Mencius, the Confucian philosopher and author of the *Mencius*.
  • 仁政必自经界始 - Direct quote from the *Mencius*, emphasizing that benevolent governance must begin with the proper demarcation of land.
  • 三千之徒 - Refers to Mencius's disciples or to the disciples of Confucius, depending on context.
  • 顔子黙 - Reference to Yan Hui, one of Confucius's most esteemed disciples, who was known for his silence and humility.

Proper Nouns

  • 徽宗 - Emperor Huizong of Song.
  • 绍兴 - The Shaoxing era (1131–1162) of the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 李椿年 - Li Chunnian, a Song dynasty official.
  • 孟子 - Mencius, the Confucian philosopher.
  • 颜子 - Yan Hui, a disciple of Confucius.
  • 经界量田法 - The 'land boundary and measurement law' or 'jingjie liangtian' system.
  • 安济坊 - A public medical institution.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 九室宫 - Likely a Daoist monastery or retreat located in the mountains, associated with Chen Xi Yi's teachings or visits.

  • 卧阁 - A term for a chamber or room where a recluse sleeps, often used in Daoist or Buddhist contexts.
  • 耕牛 - Oxen used for plowing, a key asset for farmers in ancient China. The number of oxen was a measure of wealth and productivity.
  • 墓田 - A burial field or family graveyard, often maintained by descendants and associated with ancestral veneration.
  • 乡人 - Villagers or local people, indicating the social context of the story.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 呼之曰其说是矣 - A phrase beginning with a direct address, likely 'He called out, 'This is correct!' or 'He said, 'This is right!'' – elliptical structure, responding to a previous statement.
  • 我少在汝家时 - Means 'When I was young in your house' – a common way to refer to an earlier period of residence or relationship.
  • 必使我辈伴服一粒 - Means 'He would have us accompany him in taking one pill' – '我辈' is a humble plural form meaning 'we' or 'us.'
  • 积久数多故储蓄毒根 - Means 'Accumulated over time in large quantities, thus storing a toxic root' – a metaphorical expression indicating the buildup of harmful effects from long-term use of a drug.
  • 张谢去章母旋以此终 - 'Zhang and Xie went away, and Lady Zhang soon died of this' – '旋以此终' is a concise way of saying 'soon died as a result of this.'
  • 项生一疮甚恶 - 'Xiang developed a wound that was very evil' – '甚恶' is a strong descriptor, meaning 'very bad' or 'extremely foul.'
  • 村医为灼艾 - 'The village doctor burned moxa (mugwort)' – '灼艾' refers to the traditional Chinese practice of moxibustion.
  • 俄努肉隆起如卷 - 'Soon the flesh swelled up like a scroll' – '卷' (scroll) is used metaphorically to describe a raised, rolled-up shape.
  • 颇卷成花蕚 - 'It gradually rolled into the shape of a flower's calyx' – '花蕚' is the calyx of a flower, indicating a specific botanical shape.
  • 或误为物触则痛彻心膂 - 'Sometimes it was mistaken for an object that had touched it, causing pain to the heart and limbs' – '心膂' refers to the heart and limbs, often used metaphorically for the entire body.
  • 此名翻花脑痔 - 'This is called 'Fanhua Naohizi'' – a medical term, likely a specific type of abscess or ulcer.
  • 世人患者絶少吾方书亦不载治法 - 'Very few people in the world suffer from this; my medical texts do not record a treatment method' – a formal way of expressing that the condition is rare and undocumented.
  • 舍之而去 - 'Left it behind and went away' – a common expression of giving up on something.
  • 复涂药线系托半日许 - 'Again applied medicinal thread and tied it for about half a day' – '系托' is a medical term for tying or securing a thread for treatment.
  • 卷随线堕然转手再结至于四五 - 'The swelling fell with the thread, but upon turning the hand, it reformed into a knot, repeating this up to four or five times' – a detailed description of a failed treatment.
  • 讫不痊凡数旬妾竟死 - 'Finally, it did not heal after several weeks, and the concubine died' – '讫不痊' means 'finally did not heal.'
  • 张花项戚方尤凶虐 - 'Zhang Huaxiang and Qi Fang were especially cruel and violent' – '尤凶虐' is a strong expression indicating extreme cruelty.
  • 张破池州驻军于教场所掠妇女无数 - 'Zhang broke into Chizhou, stationed troops at the school, and seized countless women' – '教场' is a military or training ground, often used as a location for troops.
  • 谕其徒曰各纳脚子 - 'He instructed his followers, saying: 'Each of you must provide a pair of feet'' – '脚子' is a term for a pair of feet, likely a euphemistic or metaphorical expression.
  • 双足垜叠于庭 - 'Their feet were stacked in the courtyard' – '垜叠' is a rare term meaning to pile or stack.
  • 去刖者未即死则呌呼宛转经日乃絶 - 'Those who had their feet cut off but did not die immediately cried out in agony for days before dying' – '刖' is a form of punishment, amputation.
  • 广徳尽戕官吏不遗余 - 'In Guangde, he killed all officials, leaving none behind' – '戕' means to kill or destroy.
  • 循王与之苦战二盗力不能敌始就擒 - 'Xun Wang fought against them fiercely, but the two bandits could not withstand the force and were finally captured' – '就擒' is a standard expression for being captured.
  • 责其罪戚曰主此众 - 'Xun Wang accused him of his crimes, and Qi said, 'I am the leader of these people'' – '主此众' means 'to be the leader of these people.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 黄巢庙不知何时何人所立 - This sentence is elliptical, but it means that the temple dedicated to Huang Chao is unknown when and by whom it was built.
  • 山上有黄巢庙 - This phrase can be read as 'there is a temple dedicated to Huang Chao on the mountain.'
  • 临欲仆有大黒蛇长丈许顶上髪呀然跃出为抟噬之状 - This complex sentence describes a large black snake with hair on its head leaping out in an aggressive posture just before it was about to fall.
  • 博学求之，无不利于事也 - This clause means that broad learning from many sources is beneficial in all matters.
  • 一年好处 - This phrase can be read as 'the best time of the year' or 'a good thing that happens once a year.'
  • 颇为春竒时 - This phrase means 'quite unique in spring.'
  • 殆成防云 - This phrase means 'it seems like fate.'

  • 医涂药线系瘤际再匝缚其末 - Applied medicine and tied the tumor with a thread.
  • 剪断之而出憩外舍 - Cut it off and rested in an outer room.
  • 逾两时久系处痛甚至龁齩衫袖弗堪忍 - After more than two hours, the pain became so severe that he bit and gnawed at his sleeves, unable to bear it.
  • 呼其子去线曰宁逐日受苦此痛殆彻骨髓 - Called his son to remove the thread and said, 'Rather than suffer daily, this pain almost penetrates the marrow.'
  • 子将奉戒而断线无余地欲施手不得 - His son was about to follow the instructions but found no more space to cut the thread and could not proceed.
  • 方冬月因卧火阁席上遂熟睡 - Fell asleep on a heated mat in the winter month.
  • 及醒枕畔皆如水沾湿有皮囊一片在傍 - When he woke up, the pillow beside him was as wet as water, with a piece of skin nearby.
  • 扪其瘤已不见诸子秉烛就视脑外略无瘢痕 - When he touched the tumor, it was gone. His son lit a candle to examine it, and there were no scars on the outside of his head.
  • 盖附着成赘初不相干也 - It was attached and formed a growth, but initially it was not related to anything.

  • 临终与母诀曰此宅蹀已久必生变怪宜急徙以避之 - Describes the sister, on her deathbed, telling her mother that the house has been cursed for a long time and that they should move quickly to avoid bad luck.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 畏他日當復見汝 - Literal meaning: 'fear that one day you will see me again.' Implies a sense of dread or anticipation of a future encounter.
  • 自居于此 - Literal meaning: 'reside here.' In context, it means to be confined or trapped in a certain state.
  • 茫不知晦明寒暖風雷霜雪之變 - Compound phrase describing a state of unawareness of natural changes, including day and night, cold and warmth, wind, thunder, frost, and snow.
  • 人間得防去取欣戚之累 - Refers to the burdens of human life, including the ability to choose and the emotional ups and downs (joy and sorrow).
  • 方翺翔廣漢之鄉莽蒼之野 - Describes a state of free and unrestrained movement in vast, open spaces.
  • 寤而偶俗 - Literal meaning: 'awake and encounter the俗.' Means to wake up and interact with ordinary people or the mundane world.
  • 讵可見吾師邪 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how can one see my teacher now?'
  • 道流驚異 - Refers to the reaction of Daoist practitioners, who are surprised or amazed.
  • 延致齋室 - Means 'invite to a meditation room' or 'bring to a place of worship.'

  • 盥沐謹慎 - Means 'wash and be cautious.' Describes a ritual of purification and careful behavior.
  • 鄉閭奔趨浸浸聞于郡邑 - Describes the gradual spread of news or rumors from the local level to the county or city.
  • 金陵守備禮迎置館舍 - Refers to the respectful reception and accommodation provided by the military or local authorities in Nanjing.
  • 達官搢紳日造謁曲拳跽拜以祈養生之方 - Describes the actions of high-ranking officials and scholars who come daily to pay respects and seek advice on health and longevity.
  • 流傳他 - Means 'spread to other regions.'
  • 聞者以不得見為恨 - Refers to those who hear about the person and feel regret for not being able to meet them.

  • 間有遺之果實者稍取啖食 - Refers to occasionally accepting fruits or food that are given.
  • 或饋以素羞亦喜而弗拒 - Means that if offered simple food, the person would be pleased but not refuse it.
  • 衣衾錢帛到輒受之 - Describes the acceptance of clothing, bedding, money, and silk without hesitation.
  • 至于服靡囓肥耽嗜醇酒 - Refers to indulging in luxurious clothing, rich food, and strong alcohol.
  • 情欲所肆漫不省擇 - Describes the unrestrained indulgence in desires without discrimination or consideration.
  • 未幾病死略無他異 - Means that after a short time, the person died without any significant changes or symptoms.
  • 上官彦衡作傳書其事 - Refers to the historian Shangguan Yanheng writing a biography or account of the event.
  • 嘆其以俄頃之間棄累年之養畔師教捐初心 - A conclusion that expresses regret over the person's abandonment of years of cultivation, departure from the teachings of their teacher, and loss of their original intentions in a short period of time.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 畏他日當復見汝自居于此: The sentence structure is somewhat elliptical, but it can be interpreted as 'fearing that one day you will see me again, I am confined here.'
  • 茫不知晦明寒暖風雷霜雪之變人間得防去取欣戚之累: This long compound phrase describes a state of unawareness of natural changes and human life's burdens. It can be broken down into smaller, more manageable parts for clarity.
  • 方翺翔廣漢之鄉莽蒼之野倉倉然如偃于巨室而无所覺: This metaphorical description can be parsed as 'free and unrestrained in vast, open spaces, like lying in a large room without feeling anything.'
  • 寤而偶俗讵可見吾師邪: The rhetorical question can be interpreted as 'awake and interacting with the mundane world, how can one see my teacher now?'
  • 道流驚異延致齋室朝夕奉香火盥沐謹慎鄉閭奔趨浸浸聞于郡邑: This long sequence describes the reaction of Daoist practitioners and the spread of news from the local level to the county. It can be broken down into smaller clauses for clarity.
  • 金陵守備禮迎置館舍達官搢紳日造謁曲拳跽拜以祈養生之方: This sequence describes the respectful reception and actions of high-ranking officials and scholars. It can be broken down into smaller clauses for clarity.
  • 流傳他聞者以不得見為恨此人本無悟入歲余志日驕汰: This sequence describes the spread of news and the person's gradual decline. It can be broken down into smaller clauses for clarity.
  • 間有遺之果實者稍取啖食或饋以素羞亦喜而弗拒衣衾錢帛到輒受之: This sequence describes the person's acceptance of food and gifts. It can be broken down into smaller clauses for clarity.
  • 至于服靡囓肥耽嗜醇酒情欲所肆漫不省擇未幾病死略無他異: This sequence describes the person's indulgence and eventual death. It can be broken down into smaller clauses for clarity.
  • 上官彦衡作傳書其事嘆其以俄頃之間棄累年之養畔師教捐初心: This sequence describes the historian's account and conclusion. It can be broken down into smaller clauses for clarity.
